name: Tuonetar
description: Tuonetar, also known as the Queen of the Underworld, is a significant figure in Finnish mythology. She reigns over Tuonela, the land of the dead, a realm often depicted as a dark and sorrowful place. Tuonetar's role extends beyond simply ruling; she embodies the very essence of death and the transition from life to the afterlife. She is not necessarily malevolent, but rather a powerful and unavoidable force. She is responsible for welcoming the souls of the departed into her kingdom. In some accounts, she is described as a formidable figure, possessing an aura of dread and wielding considerable power over the spirits residing in Tuonela. Her daughters are also important figures, often associated with disease and various afflictions, highlighting the grim nature of her dominion. Tuonetar's existence underscores the Finnish worldview's emphasis on the cyclical nature of life and death, and the inevitability of mortality.
subtitle: Queen of the Underworld

keyMyths.title: Väinämöinen's Journey to Tuonela
keyMyths.description: In the Kalevala, the epic poem of Finland, the hero Väinämöinen journeys to Tuonela to seek knowledge of the dead. He crosses the dark river, meets Tuonetar, and attempts to learn from the deceased. However, he is ultimately unsuccessful in gaining any significant wisdom, as Tuonetar prevents him from truly communing with the spirits. This myth highlights the difficulty and danger of attempting to penetrate the veil between the living and the dead, and Tuonetar's role as the gatekeeper.

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Diseases
keyMyths.description: Tuonetar is often associated with the origin of various diseases and ailments. Her daughters are sometimes described as the embodiments of these illnesses, sent forth from Tuonela to plague the living. This connection reflects the ancient Finnish belief that sickness and death were closely linked, both originating from the realm of the dead. The myth emphasizes the pervasive influence of Tuonetar's domain on the mortal world and the constant threat of disease and death.

keyMyths.title: Crossing the River of the Dead
keyMyths.description: A central aspect of Tuonela is the river that must be crossed to reach it. This river is often described as dark, treacherous, and filled with sharp objects. Tuonetar, or one of her daughters, is responsible for ferrying the souls of the deceased across this river. The journey across the river symbolizes the final transition from the world of the living to the realm of the dead, and Tuonetar's role as the guide and guardian of this passage.

sources.author: Martti Haavio
